20: }
21: 
22: export interface SessionManagerEvents {
23: 	/** Broadcast this message to all connected clients */
24: 	broadcast: [RelayMessage];
25: 	/** Send to a specific client */
26: 	send: [{ clientId: string; message: RelayMessage }];
27: 	/** Active session changed */
28: 	session_changed: [{ sessionId: string }];
29: }
30: 
31: export interface HistoryPage {
32: 	messages: Message[];
33: 	hasMore: boolean;
34: 	/** Total messages in the session (if known) */
35: 	total?: number;
36: }
37: 
38: // ─── Session Manager ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
39: 
40: export class SessionManager extends EventEmitter<SessionManagerEvents> {
41: 	private readonly client: OpenCodeClient;
42: 	private readonly historyPageSize: number;
43: 	private activeSessionId: string | null = null;
44: 
45: 	constructor(options: SessionManagerOptions) {
46: 		super();
47: 		this.client = options.client;
48: 		this.historyPageSize = options.historyPageSize ?? 50;
49: 	}
50: 
51: 	// ─── Queries ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
52: 
53: 	/** Get the active session ID */
54: 	getActiveSessionId(): string | null {
55: 		return this.activeSessionId;
56: 	}
57: 
58: 	/** List sessions sorted by most recently updated first */
59: 	async listSessions(): Promise<SessionInfo[]> {
60: 		const sessions = await this.client.listSessions();
61: 		return toSessionInfoList(sessions);
62: 	}
63: 
64: 	/**
65: 	 * Load a page of message history for a session.
66: 	 *
67: 	 * Messages are returned in chronological order (oldest first).
68: 	 * Pagination loads from the END (most recent) backwards:
69: 	 *   offset=0  → most recent pageSize messages
70: 	 *   offset=50 → the 50 messages before those
71: 	 *
72: 	 * This matches chat UI expectations: show newest messages first,
73: 	 * lazy-load older messages as the user scrolls up.
74: 	 */
75: 	async loadHistory(sessionId: string, offset = 0): Promise<HistoryPage> {
76: 		const all = await this.client.getMessages(sessionId);
77: 		const total = all.length;
78: 
79: 		// Slice from the end, moving backwards by `offset`
80: 		const end = Math.max(0, total - offset);
81: 		const start = Math.max(0, end - this.historyPageSize);
82: 		const page = all.slice(start, end);
83: 
84: 		return {
85: 			messages: page,
86: 			hasMore: start > 0,
87: 			total,
88: 		};
89: 	}
90: 
91: 	// ─── Mutations ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
92: 
93: 	/** Create a new session and make it active */
94: 	async createSession(title?: string): Promise<SessionDetail> {
95: 		const session = await this.client.createSession({
96: 			title: title ?? "New Session",
97: 		});
98: 		this.activeSessionId = session.id;
99: 
100: 		this.emit("session_changed", { sessionId: session.id });
101: 		this.emit("broadcast", { type: "session_switched", id: session.id });
102: 		await this.broadcastSessionList();
103: 
104: 		return session;
105: 	}
106: 
107: 	/**
108: 	 * Switch to an existing session.
109: 	 *
110: 	 * Does NOT broadcast session_switched — the caller (relay-stack.ts) handles
111: 	 * that with the combined protocol (events or REST API fallback inline).
112: 	 * Emits session_changed so translator resets and SSE filter updates.
113: 	 */
114: 	async switchSession(sessionId: string): Promise<void> {
115: 		this.activeSessionId = sessionId;
116: 		this.emit("session_changed", { sessionId });
117: 	}
118: 
119: 	/** Delete a session. If it was active, switch to the most recent remaining. */
120: 	async deleteSession(sessionId: string): Promise<void> {
121: 		await this.client.deleteSession(sessionId);
122: 
123: 		if (this.activeSessionId === sessionId) {
124: 			// Switch to most recent remaining session
125: 			const remaining = await this.listSessions();
126: 			if (remaining.length > 0) {
127: 				this.activeSessionId = remaining[0]!.id;
128: 				this.emit("broadcast", {
129: 					type: "session_switched",
130: 					id: this.activeSessionId,
131: 				});
132: 				this.emit("session_changed", { sessionId: this.activeSessionId });
133: 			} else {
134: 				this.activeSessionId = null;
135: 			}
136: 		}
137: 
138: 		await this.broadcastSessionList();
139: 	}
140: 
141: 	/** Rename a session */
142: 	async renameSession(sessionId: string, title: string): Promise<void> {
143: 		await this.client.updateSession(sessionId, { title });
144: 		await this.broadcastSessionList();
145: 	}
146: 
147: 	/** Search sessions by query */
148: 	async searchSessions(query: string): Promise<SessionInfo[]> {
149: 		// OpenCode supports search via query param
150: 		const sessions = await this.client.listSessions();
151: 		// Client-side filter since OpenCode's list endpoint may not support search directly
152: 		const q = query.toLowerCase();
153: 		const matches = sessions.filter((s) => {
154: 			return (
155: 				(s.title ?? "").toLowerCase().includes(q) ||
156: 				s.id.toLowerCase().includes(q)
157: 			);
158: 		});
159: 		return toSessionInfoList(matches);
160: 	}
161: 
162: 	/**
163: 	 * Initialize: always create a fresh session.
164: 	 * Previous sessions remain in the list for users to switch to, but a new
165: 	 * session ensures a clean slate (no stuck messages from prior model mismatches).
166: 	 */
167: 	async initialize(title?: string): Promise<string> {
168: 		const session = await this.client.createSession({
169: 			title: title ?? "Relay Session",
170: 		});
171: 		this.activeSessionId = session.id;
172: 		return this.activeSessionId;
173: 	}
174: 
175: 	/** Set active session directly (e.g., for SSE filter coordination) */
176: 	setActiveSessionId(sessionId: string): void {
177: 		this.activeSessionId = sessionId;
178: 	}
179: 
180: 	// ─── Internal ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
181: 
182: 	private async broadcastSessionList(): Promise<void> {
183: 		const sessions = await this.listSessions();
184: 		this.emit("broadcast", { type: "session_list", sessions });
185: 	}
186: }
187: 
188: // ─── Helpers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
189: 
190: /** Convert OpenCode SessionDetail[] → sorted SessionInfo[] for the frontend */
191: function toSessionInfoList(sessions: SessionDetail[]): SessionInfo[] {
192: 	return sessions
193: 		.map((s) => ({
194: 			id: s.id,
195: 			title: s.title ?? "Untitled",
196: 			updatedAt: s.time?.updated ?? s.time?.created ?? 0,
197: 			messageCount: 0, // OpenCode doesn't include this in list; frontend can fetch if needed
198: 		}))
199: 		.sort((a, b) => b.updatedAt - a.updatedAt);
200: }

3: import { formatErrorDetail, RelayError } from "../errors.js";
4: import type { HandlerDeps } from "./types.js";
5: 
6: export async function handleNewSession(
7: 	deps: HandlerDeps,
8: 	clientId: string,
9: 	payload: Record<string, unknown>,
10: ): Promise<void> {
11: 	const title = String(payload.title ?? "New Session");
12: 	const session = await deps.sessionMgr.createSession(title);
13: 	deps.overrides.clear();
14: 	deps.log(`   [session] client=${clientId} Created: ${session.id}`);
15: }
16: 
17: /**
18:  * Switch to a different session.
19:  *
20:  * Decision tree for history delivery:
21:  *   1. Cache has chat content (user_message or delta)?
22:  *      → Send session_switched with events (client replays)
23:  *   2. No cache? REST API fallback
24:  *      → Send session_switched with history (structured messages)
25:  *   3. REST API fails?
26:  *      → Send session_switched with no data (empty session view)
27:  *
28:  * After switch: load model info from session, clear overrides.
29:  */
30: export async function handleSwitchSession(
31: 	deps: HandlerDeps,
32: 	clientId: string,
33: 	payload: Record<string, unknown>,
34: ): Promise<void> {
35: 	const id = String(payload.sessionId ?? "");
36: 	if (!id) return;
37: 
38: 	await deps.sessionMgr.switchSession(id);
39: 	deps.overrides.clear();
40: 
41: 	// Combined protocol: include events or history in session_switched
42: 	// message (single atomic WS message, eliminates $effect race condition).
43: 	const events = deps.messageCache.getEvents(id);
44: 	const hasChatContent =
45: 		events?.some((e) => e.type === "user_message" || e.type === "delta") ??
46: 		false;
47: 	deps.log(
48: 		`   [switch] client=${clientId} session=${id} cacheEvents=${events?.length ?? "null"} hasChatContent=${hasChatContent}${events ? ` types=[${[...new Set(events.map((e) => e.type))]}]` : ""}`,
49: 	);
50: 	if (events && hasChatContent) {
51: 		// Cache hit with content: include raw events for client replay
52: 		deps.wsHandler.broadcast({
53: 			type: "session_switched",
54: 			id,
55: 			events,
56: 		});
57: 	} else {
58: 		// Cache miss (session from before relay started): REST API fallback
59: 		try {
60: 			const history = await deps.sessionMgr.loadHistory(id);
61: 			deps.log(
62: 				`   [switch] client=${clientId} session=${id} REST fallback: ${history.messages.length} messages, hasMore=${history.hasMore}`,
63: 			);
64: 			deps.wsHandler.broadcast({
65: 				type: "session_switched",
66: 				id,
67: 				history: {
68: 					messages: history.messages,
69: 					hasMore: history.hasMore,
70: 					total: history.total,
71: 				},
72: 			});
73: 		} catch {
74: 			// If REST API also fails, send switch without data
75: 			deps.wsHandler.broadcast({
76: 				type: "session_switched",
77: 				id,
78: 			});
79: 		}
80: 	}
81: 
82: 	try {
83: 		const session = await deps.client.getSession(id);
84: 		if (session.modelID) {
85: 			deps.wsHandler.broadcast({
86: 				type: "model_info",
87: 				model: session.modelID,
88: 				provider: session.providerID ?? "",
89: 			});
90: 		}
91: 	} catch (err) {
92: 		deps.log(
93: 			`   [session] client=${clientId} session=${id} Failed to load model info: ${formatErrorDetail(err)}`,
94: 		);
95: 		deps.wsHandler.sendTo(
96: 			clientId,
97: 			RelayError.fromCaught(
98: 				err,
99: 				"SESSION_ERROR",
100: 				`Failed to load model info for session ${id}`,
101: 			).toMessage(),
102: 		);
103: 	}
104: 	deps.log(`   [session] client=${clientId} Switched: ${id}`);
105: }
106: 
107: export async function handleDeleteSession(
108: 	deps: HandlerDeps,
109: 	clientId: string,
110: 	payload: Record<string, unknown>,
111: ): Promise<void> {

113: 	if (id) {

115: 		await deps.sessionMgr.deleteSession(id);

117: 	}
118: }

<content>1: // ─── Relay Harness ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // Starts the real relay stack pointed at a real OpenCode instance.
3: // Integration tests use this to exercise the exact same wiring as production.
4: //
5: // Model selection (avoids broken/quota-exhausted default models):
6: //   1. Explicit env vars: INTEGRATION_MODEL + INTEGRATION_PROVIDER
7: //   2. Fallback env vars: E2E_MODEL + E2E_PROVIDER (shared with Playwright)
8: //   3. Auto-detect: scans model_list for known free-tier models
9: 
10: import WebSocket from "ws";
11: import {
12: 	createRelayStack,
13: 	type RelayStack,
14: } from "../../../src/lib/relay-stack.js";
15: import { TestWsClient } from "./test-ws-client.js";
16: 
17: const OPENCODE_URL = process.env.OPENCODE_URL ?? "http://localhost:4096";
18: 
19: // Model override from env vars (integration-specific takes priority over shared E2E)
20: const ENV_MODEL = process.env.INTEGRATION_MODEL ?? process.env.E2E_MODEL ?? "";
21: const ENV_PROVIDER =
22: 	process.env.INTEGRATION_PROVIDER ?? process.env.E2E_PROVIDER ?? "";
23: 
24: /** Known free-tier models, in priority order (provider: model) */
25: const FREE_MODELS: Array<{ modelId: string; providerId: string }> = [
26: 	// OpenCode Zen free-tier models (no quota limits)
27: 	{ modelId: "gpt-5-nano", providerId: "opencode" },
28: 	{ modelId: "glm-5-free", providerId: "opencode" },
29: 	{ modelId: "minimax-m2.5-free", providerId: "opencode" },
30: 	{ modelId: "trinity-large-preview-free", providerId: "opencode" },
31: 	// Google free-tier models
32: 	{ modelId: "gemini-2.0-flash", providerId: "google" },
33: 	{ modelId: "gemini-2.0-flash-lite", providerId: "google" },
34: 	{ modelId: "gemini-1.5-flash", providerId: "google" },
35: 	// GitHub Copilot flash models (lower quota usage)
36: 	{ modelId: "gemini-3-flash-preview", providerId: "github-copilot" },
37: 	{ modelId: "gpt-5-mini", providerId: "github-copilot" },
38: ];
39: 
40: export interface RelayHarness {
41: 	stack: RelayStack;
42: 	opencodeUrl: string;
43: 	relayPort: number;
44: 	relayBaseUrl: string;
45: 
46: 	/** The model/provider the harness switched to (empty if none) */
47: 	model: string;
48: 	provider: string;
49: 
50: 	/** Track session IDs created during tests for cleanup */
51: 	createdSessionIds: string[];
52: 
53: 	/** Connect a test WebSocket client to the relay */
54: 	connectWsClient(): Promise<TestWsClient>;
55: 
56: 	/** Switch the relay to a specific model (for tests that reset model state) */
57: 	switchModel(modelId: string, providerId: string): Promise<void>;
58: 
59: 	/**
60: 	 * Delete all sessions created during tests (tracked via createdSessionIds).
61: 	 * Called automatically by stop(), but can be called manually if needed.
62: 	 */
63: 	cleanupSessions(): Promise<void>;
64: 
65: 	/** Stop the relay and clean up */
66: 	stop(): Promise<void>;
67: }
68: 
69: /**
70:  * Check if OpenCode is reachable. Returns true if it is, false if not.
71:  * Integration tests should skip (not fail) when OpenCode isn't running.
72:  */
73: export async function isOpenCodeRunning(url?: string): Promise<boolean> {
74: 	try {
75: 		const res = await fetch(`${url ?? OPENCODE_URL}/path`, {
76: 			signal: AbortSignal.timeout(3000),
77: 		});
78: 		return res.ok;
79: 	} catch {
80: 		return false;
81: 	}
82: }
83: 
84: /**
85:  * Send a switch_model message via a temporary WS connection.
86:  * Sets the model for all subsequent prompts from any client on this relay.
87:  */
88: async function switchModelViaWs(
89: 	relayPort: number,
90: 	modelId: string,
91: 	providerId: string,
92: ): Promise<void> {
93: 	return new Promise<void>((resolve, reject) => {
94: 		const ws = new WebSocket(`ws://127.0.0.1:${relayPort}/ws`);
95: 		const timer = setTimeout(() => {
96: 			ws.close();
97: 			reject(new Error("Timeout switching model"));
98: 		}, 5000);
99: 
100: 		ws.on("open", () => {
101: 			ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: "switch_model", modelId, providerId }));
102: 			// Give the relay a moment to process, then close
103: 			setTimeout(() => {
104: 				clearTimeout(timer);
105: 				ws.close();
106: 				resolve();
107: 			}, 300);
108: 		});
109: 
110: 		ws.on("error", (err) => {
111: 			clearTimeout(timer);
112: 			reject(err);
113: 		});
114: 	});
115: }
116: 
117: /**
118:  * Auto-detect a free-tier model from the relay's model_list.
119:  * Connects a temporary WS client, reads the model_list, and returns the
120:  * first match from FREE_MODELS that's actually available.
121:  */
122: async function autoDetectFreeModel(
123: 	relayPort: number,
124: ): Promise<{ modelId: string; providerId: string } | null> {
125: 	return new Promise((resolve) => {
126: 		const ws = new WebSocket(`ws://127.0.0.1:${relayPort}/ws`);
127: 		const timer = setTimeout(() => {
128: 			ws.close();
129: 			resolve(null);
130: 		}, 5000);
131: 
132: 		ws.on("message", (data) => {
133: 			try {
134: 				const msg = JSON.parse(data.toString());
135: 				if (msg.type !== "model_list") return;
136: 
137: 				const providers = msg.providers as
138: 					| Array<{
139: 							id: string;
140: 							configured: boolean;
141: 							models: Array<{ id: string }>;
142: 					  }>
143: 					| undefined;
144: 				if (!providers) return;
145: 
146: 				// Build a set of available model keys: "providerId:modelId"
147: 				const available = new Set<string>();
148: 				for (const p of providers) {
149: 					if (!p.configured) continue;
150: 					for (const m of p.models) {
151: 						available.add(`${p.id}:${m.id}`);
152: 					}
153: 				}
154: 
155: 				// Find first free model that's available
156: 				for (const fm of FREE_MODELS) {
157: 					if (available.has(`${fm.providerId}:${fm.modelId}`)) {
158: 						clearTimeout(timer);
159: 						ws.close();
160: 						resolve(fm);
161: 						return;
162: 					}
163: 				}
164: 
165: 				// No free model found
166: 				clearTimeout(timer);
167: 				ws.close();
168: 				resolve(null);
169: 			} catch {
170: 				// Ignore parse errors
171: 			}
172: 		});
173: 
174: 		ws.on("error", () => {
175: 			clearTimeout(timer);
176: 			resolve(null);
177: 		});
178: 	});
179: }
180: 
181: /**
182:  * Create a relay harness connected to the real OpenCode instance.
183:  * Uses port 0 (OS-assigned) to avoid conflicts.
184:  *
185:  * Automatically switches to a free-tier model to avoid quota-exhausted
186:  * default models. Priority: env vars > auto-detect from model_list.
187:  */
188: export async function createRelayHarness(opts?: {
189: 	opencodeUrl?: string;
190: }): Promise<RelayHarness> {
191: 	const opencodeUrl = opts?.opencodeUrl ?? OPENCODE_URL;
192: 
193: 	const stack = await createRelayStack({
194: 		port: 0,
195: 		host: "127.0.0.1",
196: 		opencodeUrl,
197: 		projectDir: process.cwd(),
198: 		slug: "integration-test",
199: 		sessionTitle: "Integration Test Session",
200: 		log: () => {}, // Silence relay logs during tests
201: 	});
202: 
203: 	const relayPort = stack.getPort();
204: 	const relayBaseUrl = `http://127.0.0.1:${relayPort}`;
205: 	const clients: TestWsClient[] = [];
206: 
207: 	// Switch to a free-tier model to avoid quota/broken-model failures
208: 	let model = ENV_MODEL;
209: 	let provider = ENV_PROVIDER;
210: 
211: 	if (model && provider) {
212: 		// Explicit env var — use it directly
213: 		await switchModelViaWs(relayPort, model, provider);
214: 	} else {
215: 		// Auto-detect: scan model_list for known free models
216: 		const detected = await autoDetectFreeModel(relayPort);
217: 		if (detected) {
218: 			await switchModelViaWs(relayPort, detected.modelId, detected.providerId);
219: 			model = detected.modelId;
220: 			provider = detected.providerId;
221: 		}
222: 	}
223: 
224: 	const createdSessionIds: string[] = [];
225: 
226: 	const harness: RelayHarness = {
227: 		stack,
228: 		opencodeUrl,
229: 		relayPort,
230: 		relayBaseUrl,
231: 		model,
232: 		provider,
233: 		createdSessionIds,
234: 
235: 		async connectWsClient(): Promise<TestWsClient> {
236: 			const client = new TestWsClient(`ws://127.0.0.1:${relayPort}/ws`);
237: 			clients.push(client);
238: 			await client.waitForOpen();
239: 			return client;
240: 		},
241: 
242: 		async switchModel(modelId: string, providerId: string): Promise<void> {
243: 			await switchModelViaWs(relayPort, modelId, providerId);
244: 		},
245: 
246: 		async cleanupSessions(): Promise<void> {
247: 			if (createdSessionIds.length === 0) return;
248: 			try {
249: 				const client = new TestWsClient(`ws://127.0.0.1:${relayPort}/ws`);
250: 				await client.waitForOpen();
251: 				for (const sessionId of createdSessionIds) {
252: 					client.send({ type: "delete_session", sessionId });
253: 				}
254: 				// Give the deletes a moment to process
255: 				await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, 500));
256: 				await client.close();
257: 				createdSessionIds.length = 0;
258: 			} catch {
259: 				// Best-effort cleanup
260: 			}
261: 		},
262: 
263: 		async stop(): Promise<void> {
264: 			for (const c of clients) {
265: 				await c.close().catch(() => {});
266: 			}
267: 			// Clean up sessions created during tests
268: 			await harness.cleanupSessions();
269: 			await stack.stop();
270: 		},
271: 	};
272: 
273: 	return harness;
274: }

2: // Create and clean up test sessions for contract tests.

4: import { apiDelete, apiGet, apiPost } from "./server-connection.js";
5: 
6: export interface TestSession {

9: 	title?: string;
10: 	projectID?: string;
11: 	directory?: string;
12: 	version?: string;
13: 	time?: { created?: number; updated?: number };
14: }
15: 
16: /**
17:  * Create a test session with a recognizable title for cleanup.
18:  */
19: export async function createTestSession(
20: 	title = `contract-test-${Date.now()}`,
21: ): Promise<TestSession> {
22: 	return apiPost<TestSession>("/session", { title });
23: }
24: 
25: /**
26:  * Delete a test session, ignoring errors (best-effort cleanup).
27:  */
28: export async function deleteTestSession(sessionId: string): Promise<void> {
29: 	try {
30: 		await apiDelete(`/session/${sessionId}`);
31: 	} catch {
32: 		// Ignore — cleanup is best effort
33: 	}
34: }
35: 
36: /**
37:  * Get messages for a session.
38:  */
39: export async function getSessionMessages(
40: 	sessionId: string,
41: ): Promise<unknown[]> {
42: 	const res = await apiGet<unknown>(`/session/${sessionId}/message`);
43: 	if (Array.isArray(res)) return res;
44: 	if (typeof res === "object" && res !== null) {
45: 		return Object.values(res as Record<string, unknown>);
46: 	}
47: 	return [];
48: }
49: 
50: /**
51:  * Send a prompt to a session asynchronously.
52:  */
53: export async function sendPrompt(
54: 	sessionId: string,
55: 	text: string,
56: ): Promise<void> {
57: 	await apiPost(`/session/${sessionId}/prompt_async`, { text });
58: }
59: 
60: /**
61:  * Wait for a session to become idle (polling).
62:  * Returns true if idle within timeout, false otherwise.
63:  */
64: export async function waitForIdle(
65: 	_sessionId: string,
66: 	timeoutMs = 60_000,
67: 	pollMs = 500,
68: ): Promise<boolean> {
69: 	const deadline = Date.now() + timeoutMs;
70: 	while (Date.now() < deadline) {
71: 		try {
72: 			const status = await apiGet<{ status?: { type?: string } }>(
73: 				"/session/status",
74: 			);
75: 			if (status?.status?.type === "idle") return true;
76: 		} catch {
77: 			// Ignore — server might be busy
78: 		}
79: 		await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, pollMs));
80: 	}
81: 	return false;
82: }
83: 
84: /**
85:  * Collect SSE events for a given duration or until a predicate is met.
86:  */
87: export async function collectSSEEvents(
88: 	path: string,
89: 	opts: {
90: 		maxEvents?: number;
91: 		timeoutMs?: number;
92: 		until?: (event: {
93: 			type: string;
94: 			properties: Record<string, unknown>;
95: 		}) => boolean;
96: 	} = {},
97: ): Promise<Array<{ type: string; properties: Record<string, unknown> }>> {
98: 	const { maxEvents = 100, timeoutMs = 30_000, until } = opts;
99: 	const events: Array<{ type: string; properties: Record<string, unknown> }> =
100: 		[];
101: 
102: 	const controller = new AbortController();
103: 	const deadline = setTimeout(() => controller.abort(), timeoutMs);
104: 
105: 	try {
106: 		const res = await fetch(`${new URL(path, "http://localhost:4096").href}`, {
107: 			signal: controller.signal,
108: 			headers: { Accept: "text/event-stream" },
109: 		});
110: 
111: 		if (!res.ok || !res.body) return events;
112: 
113: 		const reader = res.body.getReader();
114: 		const decoder = new TextDecoder();
115: 		let buffer = "";
116: 
117: 		while (events.length < maxEvents) {
118: 			const { done, value } = await reader.read();
119: 			if (done) break;
120: 
121: 			buffer += decoder.decode(value, { stream: true });
122: 			const lines = buffer.split("\n");
123: 			buffer = lines.pop() ?? "";
124: 
125: 			for (const line of lines) {
126: 				if (line.startsWith("data: ")) {
127: 					try {
128: 						const parsed = JSON.parse(line.slice(6)) as {
129: 							type: string;
130: 							properties: Record<string, unknown>;
131: 						};
132: 						events.push(parsed);
133: 						if (until?.(parsed)) {
134: 							controller.abort();
135: 							return events;
136: 						}
137: 					} catch {
138: 						// Skip malformed data
139: 					}
140: 				}
141: 			}
142: 		}
143: 	} catch {
144: 		// AbortError expected
145: 	} finally {
146: 		clearTimeout(deadline);
147: 		controller.abort();
148: 	}
149: 
150: 	return events;
151: }

<content>1: // ─── Integration: Session Switch History ──────────────────────────────────────
2: // Verifies the bug fix: switching sessions broadcasts history_page so agent
3: // output doesn't disappear when switching away and back.
4: //
5: // Root cause: relay-stack.ts switch_session handler previously discarded the
6: // HistoryPage returned by sessionMgr.switchSession(). The fix captures and
7: // broadcasts it to all clients.
8: //
9: // Runs against REAL OpenCode — no mocks.
10: 
11: import { afterAll, beforeAll, describe, expect, it } from "vitest";
12: import {
13: 	createRelayHarness,
14: 	isOpenCodeRunning,
15: 	type RelayHarness,
16: } from "../helpers/relay-harness.js";
17: 
18: const available = await isOpenCodeRunning();
19: 
20: describe.skipIf(!available)("Integration: Session Switch History", () => {
21: 	let harness: RelayHarness;
22: 
23: 	function trackSession(id: string) {
24: 		harness.createdSessionIds.push(id);
25: 	}
26: 
27: 	beforeAll(async () => {
28: 		harness = await createRelayHarness();
29: 	}, 30_000);
30: 
31: 	afterAll(async () => {
32: 		if (harness) await harness.stop();
33: 	});
34: 
35: 	// ── Core regression: switching back to a session with messages ────────
36: 
37: 	it("switch_session broadcasts history_page with session messages", async () => {
38: 		const client = await harness.connectWsClient();
39: 		await client.waitForInitialState();
40: 
41: 		// Record the initial session (assigned on connect)
42: 		const initialSwitched = client.getReceivedOfType("session_switched");
43: 		expect(initialSwitched.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);
44: 		const sessionA = initialSwitched[0]!.id as string;
45: 
46: 		// Send a message to create conversation history in session A
47: 		client.clearReceived();
48: 		client.send({
49: 			type: "message",
50: 			text: "Reply with just the word 'pong'. Nothing else.",
51: 		});
52: 
53: 		// Wait for the full model round-trip
54: 		await client.waitFor("done", { timeout: 80_000 });
55: 		client.clearReceived();
56: 
57: 		// Create a new session (auto-switches away from session A)
58: 		client.send({ type: "new_session", title: "Switch Away Target" });
59: 		const switchedToB = await client.waitFor("session_switched", {
60: 			timeout: 5_000,
61: 		});
62: 		trackSession(switchedToB.id as string);
63: 		client.clearReceived();
64: 
65: 		// Switch back to session A
66: 		client.send({ type: "switch_session", sessionId: sessionA });
67: 
68: 		// Should receive session_switched for session A
69: 		const switchedBack = await client.waitFor("session_switched", {
70: 			timeout: 5_000,
71: 		});
72: 		expect(switchedBack.id).toBe(sessionA);
73: 
74: 		// Should receive history_page for session A (the bug fix)
75: 		const historyPage = await client.waitFor("history_page", {
76: 			timeout: 5_000,
77: 			predicate: (m) => m.sessionId === sessionA,
78: 		});
79: 
80: 		expect(historyPage.sessionId).toBe(sessionA);
81: 		expect(Array.isArray(historyPage.messages)).toBe(true);
82: 		const messages = historyPage.messages as unknown[];
83: 		// At least the user message should be in history
84: 		expect(messages.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);
85: 		expect(typeof historyPage.hasMore).toBe("boolean");
86: 
87: 		await client.close();
88: 	}, 120_000);
89: 
90: 	// ── Mid-stream switch: partial response preserved in history ─────────
91: 
92: 	it("switching away mid-stream then back preserves full conversation content", async () => {
93: 		const client = await harness.connectWsClient();
94: 		await client.waitForInitialState();
95: 
96: 		// Use the initial session — it has the free-tier model configured
97: 		// by the harness (new_session resets model selection, which can
98: 		// cause quota errors with the default model).
99: 		const initialSwitched = client.getReceivedOfType("session_switched");
100: 		expect(initialSwitched.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);
101: 		const sessionA = initialSwitched[0]!.id as string;
102: 
103: 		// Ask something that produces a longer, multi-part response.
104: 		// The prompt asks for 5 numbered items with 2–3 sentences each,
105: 		// giving us enough streaming deltas to catch one mid-flight.
106: 		const userPrompt =
107: 			"List 5 interesting facts about the number pi (π). " +
108: 			"Number each fact 1 through 5 and write 2-3 sentences for each.";
109: 
110: 		client.clearReceived();
111: 		client.send({ type: "message", text: userPrompt });
112: 
113: 		// Wait for the first streaming delta — proof the model started responding
114: 		const firstDelta = await client.waitFor("delta", {
115: 			timeout: 60_000,
116: 		});
117: 		const deltaSnippet = (firstDelta.text as string).trim();
118: 		expect(deltaSnippet.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);
119: 
120: 		// Collect a few more deltas so we have substantial streamed text
121: 		await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, 2_000));
122: 		const allDeltas = client.getReceivedOfType("delta");
123: 		const streamedText = allDeltas.map((d) => d.text as string).join("");
124: 		expect(streamedText.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);
125: 
126: 		// ── Switch away mid-stream ──────────────────────────────────────
127: 		client.send({
128: 			type: "new_session",
129: 			title: "Mid-Stream Switch Target",
130: 		});
131: 		const switchedToB = await client.waitFor("session_switched", {
132: 			timeout: 5_000,
133: 		});
134: 		trackSession(switchedToB.id as string);
135: 
136: 		// Wait long enough for the model to finish processing session A
137: 		// in the background. OpenCode continues even after relay switches.
138: 		await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, 30_000));
139: 
140: 		// ── Switch back ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
141: 		client.clearReceived();
142: 		client.send({ type: "switch_session", sessionId: sessionA });
143: 
144: 		const historyPage = await client.waitFor("history_page", {
145: 			timeout: 5_000,
146: 			predicate: (m) => m.sessionId === sessionA,
147: 		});
148: 
149: 		// ── Helpers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
150: 
151: 		// OpenCode messages may nest role in `info` or at top level
152: 		const getRole = (m: Record<string, unknown>): string => {
153: 			if (typeof m.role === "string") return m.role;
154: 			const info = m.info as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;
155: 			if (info && typeof info.role === "string") return info.role;
156: 			return "";
157: 		};
158: 
159: 		// Extract all text content from a message's parts
160: 		const getTextContent = (m: Record<string, unknown>): string => {
161: 			const parts = (m.parts ?? []) as Array<Record<string, unknown>>;
162: 			return parts
163: 				.filter((p) => p.type === "text")
164: 				.map((p) => String(p.text ?? p.content ?? ""))
165: 				.join("");
166: 		};
167: 
168: 		// ── Verify conversation structure ───────────────────────────────
169: 		const messages = historyPage.messages as Array<Record<string, unknown>>;

<content>1: // ─── Integration: Message Lifecycle ──────────────────────────────────────────
2: // Full end-to-end lifecycle test against a LIVE OpenCode server.
3: // Verifies the complete message flow that the mock-based tests couldn't catch:
4: //   send → status:processing → delta(s) → done(code:0) → idle
5: //
6: // This test would have caught the original bug where a model silently fails
7: // (no deltas, no done event, UI stuck forever).
8: 
9: import { afterAll, beforeAll, describe, expect, it } from "vitest";
10: import {
11: 	createRelayHarness,
12: 	isOpenCodeRunning,
13: 	type RelayHarness,
14: } from "../helpers/relay-harness.js";
15: 
16: const available = await isOpenCodeRunning();
17: 
18: describe.skipIf(!available)("Integration: Message Lifecycle", () => {
19: 	let harness: RelayHarness;
20: 
21: 	beforeAll(async () => {
22: 		harness = await createRelayHarness();
23: 	}, 30_000);
24: 
25: 	afterAll(async () => {
26: 		if (harness) await harness.stop();
27: 	});
28: 
29: 	it("complete lifecycle: send → processing → delta → done", async () => {
30: 		const client = await harness.connectWsClient();
31: 		await client.waitForInitialState();
32: 		client.clearReceived();
33: 
34: 		// Send a minimal prompt
35: 		client.send({
36: 			type: "message",
37: 			text: "Reply with just the word 'pong'. Nothing else.",
38: 		});
39: 
40: 		// 1. Should receive processing status
41: 		const status = await client.waitFor("status", {
42: 			predicate: (m) => m.status === "processing",
43: 			timeout: 5_000,
44: 		});
45: 		expect(status.status).toBe("processing");
46: 
47: 		// 2. Should receive at least one delta (streamed text)
48: 		const delta = await client.waitFor("delta", { timeout: 30_000 });
49: 		expect(delta.text).toBeTruthy();
50: 		expect(typeof delta.text).toBe("string");
51: 
52: 		// 3. Should receive done with code 0 (successful completion)
53: 		const done = await client.waitFor("done", { timeout: 60_000 });
54: 		expect(done.code).toBe(0);
55: 
56: 		await client.close();
57: 	}, 90_000);
58: 
59: 	it("sequential messages: second message works after first completes", async () => {
60: 		const client = await harness.connectWsClient();
61: 		await client.waitForInitialState();
62: 		client.clearReceived();
63: 
64: 		// --- First message ---
65: 		client.send({
66: 			type: "message",
67: 			text: "Reply with just 'one'.",
68: 		});
69: 
70: 		await client.waitFor("status", {
71: 			predicate: (m) => m.status === "processing",
72: 			timeout: 5_000,
73: 		});
74: 		const done1 = await client.waitFor("done", { timeout: 60_000 });
75: 		expect(done1.code).toBe(0);
76: 
77: 		// Clear messages between turns
78: 		client.clearReceived();
79: 
80: 		// --- Second message ---
81: 		client.send({
82: 			type: "message",
83: 			text: "Reply with just 'two'.",
84: 		});
85: 
86: 		// Should enter processing again (not stuck from first turn)
87: 		const status2 = await client.waitFor("status", {
88: 			predicate: (m) => m.status === "processing",
89: 			timeout: 5_000,
90: 		});
91: 		expect(status2.status).toBe("processing");
92: 
93: 		// Should receive delta for second message
94: 		const delta2 = await client.waitFor("delta", { timeout: 30_000 });
95: 		expect(delta2.text).toBeTruthy();
96: 
97: 		// Should complete
98: 		const done2 = await client.waitFor("done", { timeout: 60_000 });
99: 		expect(done2.code).toBe(0);
100: 
101: 		await client.close();
102: 	}, 120_000);
103: 
104: 	it("done event resets state — no stale processing status", async () => {
105: 		const client = await harness.connectWsClient();
106: 		await client.waitForInitialState();
107: 		client.clearReceived();
108: 
109: 		client.send({
110: 			type: "message",
111: 			text: "Reply with just 'ok'.",
112: 		});
113: 
114: 		// Wait for full cycle
115: 		await client.waitFor("done", { timeout: 60_000 });
116: 
117: 		// After done, the last status-related message should indicate idle/done
118: 		// (no lingering processing status)
119: 		const allDone = client.getReceivedOfType("done");
120: 		expect(allDone.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);
121: 		expect(allDone[allDone.length - 1]!.code).toBe(0);
122: 
123: 		await client.close();
124: 	}, 90_000);
125: });

<content>1: // ─── Integration: Cancel / Abort Lifecycle ───────────────────────────────────
2: // Tests the cancel/abort flow against a LIVE OpenCode server.
3: // Replaces the mock-based cancel-abort.test.ts with real verification:
4: //   send → processing → cancel → abort called → done → can send again
5: 
6: import { afterAll, beforeAll, describe, expect, it } from "vitest";
7: import {
8: 	createRelayHarness,
9: 	isOpenCodeRunning,
10: 	type RelayHarness,
11: } from "../helpers/relay-harness.js";
12: 
13: const available = await isOpenCodeRunning();
14: 
15: describe.skipIf(!available)("Integration: Cancel / Abort Lifecycle", () => {
16: 	let harness: RelayHarness;
17: 
18: 	beforeAll(async () => {
19: 		harness = await createRelayHarness();
20: 	}, 30_000);
21: 
22: 	afterAll(async () => {
23: 		if (harness) await harness.stop();
24: 	});
25: 
26: 	it("cancel during processing triggers done", async () => {
27: 		const client = await harness.connectWsClient();
28: 		await client.waitForInitialState();
29: 		client.clearReceived();
30: 
31: 		// Send a prompt that will take a moment to process
32: 		client.send({
33: 			type: "message",
34: 			text: "Write a short paragraph about the weather.",
35: 		});
36: 
37: 		// Wait for processing to start
38: 		await client.waitFor("status", {
39: 			predicate: (m) => m.status === "processing",
40: 			timeout: 5_000,
41: 		});
42: 
43: 		// Send cancel while processing
44: 		client.send({ type: "cancel" });
45: 
46: 		// Should receive done (code 0 from OpenCode idle, or code 1 from relay cancel)
47: 		const done = await client.waitFor("done", { timeout: 15_000 });
48: 		expect(typeof done.code).toBe("number");
49: 
50: 		await client.close();
51: 	}, 30_000);
52: 
53: 	it("can send a new message after cancel", async () => {
54: 		const client = await harness.connectWsClient();
55: 		await client.waitForInitialState();
56: 		client.clearReceived();
57: 
58: 		// First: send + cancel
59: 		client.send({
60: 			type: "message",
61: 			text: "Write a long essay about oceans.",
62: 		});
63: 
64: 		await client.waitFor("status", {
65: 			predicate: (m) => m.status === "processing",
66: 			timeout: 5_000,
67: 		});
68: 
69: 		client.send({ type: "cancel" });
70: 		await client.waitFor("done", { timeout: 15_000 });
71: 
72: 		// Let SSE stream settle — stale events from the cancelled message may
73: 		// still be in flight. Wait for OpenCode to fully return to idle.
74: 		await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, 3000));
75: 
76: 		// Clear messages between turns
77: 		client.clearReceived();
78: 
79: 		// Second: send a new message — should work (not stuck)
80: 		client.send({
81: 			type: "message",
82: 			text: "Reply with just 'ok'.",
83: 		});
84: 
85: 		// Should enter processing again
86: 		const status = await client.waitFor("status", {
87: 			predicate: (m) => m.status === "processing",
88: 			timeout: 10_000,
89: 		});
90: 		expect(status.status).toBe("processing");
91: 
92: 		// Should complete — wait for done (delta may or may not arrive
93: 		// depending on model streaming behavior after abort)
94: 		const done = await client.waitFor("done", { timeout: 90_000 });
95: 		expect(done.code).toBe(0);
96: 
97: 		await client.close();
98: 	}, 120_000);
99: 
100: 	it("cancel when idle is harmless (no crash)", async () => {
101: 		const client = await harness.connectWsClient();
102: 		await client.waitForInitialState();
103: 		client.clearReceived();
104: 
105: 		// Send cancel without having sent a message
106: 		client.send({ type: "cancel" });
107: 
108: 		// Wait a moment — should not crash or produce unexpected messages
